items.title: Phasmophobia's Biggest Update Yet Has Been Delayed, With 1.0 Now Targeting 2027
items.summary: The ghosts are waiting a little longer to scare you, as Phasmophobia 1.0 moves its release date.
items.content:
Phasmophobia's Biggest Update Yet Has Been Delayed, With 1.0 Now Targeting 2027 From TheGamer (Written by Allyson Cochran) on June 25, 2026 | OpenCritic

Phasmophobia's long-awaited 1.0 update has been delayed, with developer Kinetic Games now targeting a release window in the second half of 2027 instead of its previously announced 2026 launch.

The news was confirmed during a media presentation I attended, where marketing and partnerships director Asim Tanvir revealed the studio is pushing the release back to ensure the update launches at the quality level the team wants. The delay will also impact the game's Nintendo Switch 2 version, which is planned to release alongside 1.0.

"We're looking at shifting that to the second half of 2027," Tanvir said. "This is basically to ensure the highest quality launch when we do launch 1.0."

Kinetic Games CEO and game director Daniel Knight said the delay will give the team more time to refine the update based on community feedback, adding that the studio wants to shape Phasmophobia into "the refined horror experience our players expect." Knight also thanked the community for helping the studio grow to a team of more than 50 people and for giving the team the opportunity to continue improving the game.

While gamers will have to wait longer, Kinetic Games says the additional development time will allow the...

items.title: Phasmophobia New Roadmap for 2026 Revealed, Version 1.0 Launch Confirmed for 2027
items.summary: Phasmophobia's new roadmap for 2026 has been revealed, as the developers confirmed it'll be in version 1.0 sometime in 2027.
items.content:
While Phasmophobia was released in early access six years ago, Kinetic Games isn’t winding down support for the multiplayer horror game.

Today, Kinetic Games revealed Phasmophobioa’s new roadmap for 2026, confirming the new map rework that’s on the way and more.

Not only that, but the studio has promised that we’ll see Phasmophobia’s 1.0 launch in 2027.

The major addition this 2026 will be the rework of the 13 Willow Street map, as well as Kinetic refining core systems, improving the player experience and more.

The 13 Willow Street map rework is set to launch next month on July 21, a will include “room refreshes, tweaked hiding spots, and integrated, never-before-seen lore, and will be accompanied by the first of two major quality-of-life updates that will bring improvements to player character models, animations and customisation options.”

Expect a title update to be released on July 21, and once it’s live, you’ll see it here on MP1st.

In other horror gaming news, Hideo Kojima’s upcoming horror title “OD” will feature an in-game system that lets players continue playing even if they’re super scared.

Stay connected to MP1st and the latest news by following us on Bluesky , X , Facebook , TikTok , YouTube , and Google News .

Father, gamer, games media vet, writer of words, killer of noobs.

items.title: Phasmophobia 2026 and Beyond
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Over the last month and a half, we’ve been reading the discussions about Phasmophobia across so many different channels and taking note of the biggest issues, points of concern, questions and more. We are sorry
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. 

Over the last month and a half, we’ve been reading the discussions about Phasmophobia across so many different channels and taking note of the biggest issues, points of concern, questions and more. We are sorry it’s taken a while to respond, but we wanted to make sure that when we did, we had something of substance for you. Hopefully today, with our updated Roadmap and information on what’s to come to Phasmophobia, we can start to build back your trust and have meaningful conversations around the game we all care so deeply about.

We’ll go in to more detail on everything in this blog, but for those of you that would rather just have the headlines, here we go:

  • The rework of 13 Willow Street will launch July 21st, 2026 alongside a Quality of Life update.

    [/*]

  • A further Quality of Life update will launch in August 2026.

    [/*]

  • Our  upgrade to Unity 6 will take place in October 2026.

    [/*]

  • An additional Player Character update will launch in November, bringing with it improved animations, character models, UI, more clothing and customisation options.

    [/*]

  • Phasmophobia 1.0, as well as our next new map, will now launch in 2027.

    [/*]

A New Roadmap

Over the last month, we’ve taken a look at our plans for 2026 and made important decisions and changes to make sure we get things right. Phasmophobia’s new Roadmap, which can be seen below, focuses on bringing the game back in-line with our vision and one that we hope reignites your passion for it. 

With a focus on quality of life over new content, our various updates will bring with them hundreds of fixes - starting with our first update in July - as well as bringing the game up- to-date with Unity 6. Our second Player Character update will improve and redo areas such as the animations, character models, UI and introduce a host of new customisation options.

Alongside these various updates, you can expect to see more regular Development Previews that’ll take you deeper into what they’ll contain and the improvements they’ll bring to Phasmophobia.

You may notice that a few things have been removed from the Roadmap for 2026, such as Phasmophobia 1.0 and our new map. While we’ll talk more about the 1.0 delay further down in this blog, we want to reassure you that a new map will be coming to Phasmophobia, just a little later than planned.

For now, let’s go into a little detail on the first update - bringing with it the reworked 13 Willow Street. Willow Update

Our 13 Willow Street Rework update will launch on 21st July, 2026, bringing with it the reworked map and the first of our Quality of Life updates.

Before we get into the second part, let’s take a look at the new 13 Willow Street.

As with our rework on 6 Tanglewood Drive, this new version of 13 Willow Street retains an almost identical layout to the location currently in-game but features redesigned rooms, new theming and plenty of new interactions and objects to discover. 13 Willow Street will continue our approach to storytelling in Phasmophobia too, with a story waiting to be pieced together. We can’t wait to hear your theories and thoughts.

Quality of Life Updates Our Quality of Life updates this year will focus on bug fixes, gameplay improvements and the overall stability and playability of the game. Due to the hundreds of bug fixes, changes and new features currently planned for release, we will be releasing two separate Quality of Life updates - the first with the Willow launch and the second in August 2026. 

These updates have been built off the back of the feedback we’ve received since the launch of the Player Character Update, as well as changes we’ve wanted to implement for a long time. We’ll provide full patch notes and details at release of the updates, but here’s a few details on some of the improvements: 

  • New keybinds for pressing 1,2,3 and 4 for the individual inventory slots for keyboard and mouse only.

    [/*]

  • FOV adjustments for held items to increase item visibility.

    [/*]

  • Eye Adaptation option for console players.

    [/*]

  • Moving the Truck closer to map entrances on Prison and Brownstone High School.

    [/*]

  • Restricted map variants for Point Hope, Brownstone High School and Prison.

    [/*]

  • VR improvements; equipment usability changes, belt improvements, character movement and more.

    [/*]

  • The legacy badge screen will be available in the customisation shop to allow you to customise it whenever you want to.

    [/*]

  • The collected bone will show in the Journal.

    [/*]

  • The ghost type can be selected as a custom difficulty option.

    [/*]

  • The zoom feature in the Customisation Shop will now zoom in on the selected area.

    [/*]

  • Ability to raise the Watch while carrying equipment.

    [/*]

  • Improvements to character animations; leg movements, appearance changes when dead.

    [/*]

  • Equipment switching animation speed increases including making opening and closing the journal almost instant.

    [/*]

  • Improvements to photo detection as well as showing an indicator for what you are taking a photo of, similar to video cameras.

    [/*]

  • Equipment can be instantly used, allowing the animation to be skipped.

    [/*]

These two Quality of Life updates will address most of the issues players are encountering, and aim for them to drastically improve the state of Phasmophobia. Other planned updates, such as our move to Unity 6 later in the year, will further improve the stability and performance of the game.

Phasmophobia 1.0 Launches 2027

Earlier this year, we committed to launching Phasmophobia 1.0 at the end of 2026. Now, following a review of our plans and internal processes, we’re making the decision to move the release of 1.0 to the second half of 2027. The game will be coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 at the same time too.

We know this will come as a disappointment to many, but this is the right decision to ensure we deliver the 1.0 version of Phasmophobia that is true to our vision, high quality standards and provides the experience for players that we’ve always wanted to achieve.

We’re not going to stay quiet though. Beginning in 2027 we’ll be bringing you with us during development through regular streams and other means; showcasing our work, sharing some of the secrets of Phasmophobia 1.0 and much more. While we won’t spoil all of the surprises, we want to be as visible as possible for you, hopefully rebuilding the trust that we’ve understandably lost this year.

That said, let’s not wait until the new year to share something.

Below is our first look at the improved model for Patient 07, a fan-favourite who - along with all of our ghost models - is getting a much needed glow up in 1.0.

All of our ghost models will be given a new lease of life(?) with this visual upgrade, but will also be providing their own unique experiences for players. What that entails exactly is something we’ll go into more detail with down the line, but we look forward to sharing that with you when we’re ready to.

Another aspect of 1.0 we want to reveal today is the introduction of Conduits. Replacing the Bone as a collectable during each contract, these are unique items paired with a particular ghost model. 

A Conduit is an item that ties a ghost to our world, preventing them from moving on. These items will provide new lore to the ghost and the overall world of Phasmophobia.

Perhaps you can piece together some tidbits already? 

Rest of the Year

We’ve focused on some of this year’s major updates for Phasmophobia, but as you can see, the Roadmap features more than just these. Following on from our two Quality of Life updates, we’ll be making the jump up to Unity 6 in October. 

This Unity 6 update will provide some crucial stability updates as well as a brand new lighting system for Phasmophobia. We’ll also be implementing further accessibility options such as preferences for colour blind players as well as further quality of life improvements and bug fixes. We’ll dive deeper into these changes in a Development Preview later this year, so please do keep an eye out for that. 

Our second Player Character update will arrive in November, bringing with it brand new customisation options and cosmetics, an improved Shop UI as well as new animations and graphical improvements for our character models in-game, giving each character much more life and personality than is currently available. 

Elsewhere, we’ll see the return of Crimson Eye and Winter’s Jest (sans leaderboards) as well as our third map rework of the year, 42 Edgefield Road. Finally, we’re also planning one extra content update for the game later in 2026 that’ll bring with it changes to our Weekly Challenges ,new Voice Recognition features and more. We will share details on all of our updates when the time is right.

Moving Phasmophobia 1.0 to 2027 allows us the time and resources to get it to the high standard that you all deserve and to rebuild the relationship between Kinetic Games and you, our community. While the news of delays is never a good thing, we do hope that the information we’ve shared with you today helps you understand our decision.

We’re sorry for how Phasmophobia was left following our last update, and we understand your frustration. The Player Character Update did not meet the standards we set for ourselves or the expectations you have for the game. Our next update is the first step in a new direction, and we look forward to sharing it with you on July 21st.

Thank you, Ghost Hunters, for your time reading this blog, your passion and support for Phasmophobia and so much more.

items.title: Phasmophobia 1.0 Wants Veterans To Stop Solving Ghosts Like Spooky Math Problems
items.summary: Your ghost knowledge won’t save you forever. Phasmophobia 1.0 is rebuilding the fear from the ground up.
items.content:
By Allyson Cochran Published Jun 24, 2026, 10:01 AM EDT Allyson Cochran is an Editor at TheGamer who has worked as a journalist since 2020 and in games journalism since 2022. She holds a bachelor’s degree in journalism and focuses primarily on guides, previews, and developer interviews, with particular expertise in cozy indie games and live-service FPS titles. She has interviewed numerous game directors, including those of games like Overwatch and Dead by Daylight. Allyson has written and edited thousands of guides, and she genuinely loves every minute of it. Sign in to your TheGamer account Add Us On follow Follow followed Followed Like Like Thread Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap I’ve spent enough time with Phasmophobia over the years to know exactly where I belong during a ghost hunt: hiding in the van. This is a role that my friends, who drag me into after-hours ghost investigations, would describe as ‘not helping’. I prefer to think of it as strategic oversight.

Regardless, after seeing what developer Kinetic Games has planned for Phasmophobia 1.0 after it leaves Early Access, I may be forced to stick to my van strategy because the next era of Phasmophobia is focused on more than polishing an already successful co-op horror game, it’s about making players even more scared.

I sat down for a preview interview of the game’s full release, where marketing and partnerships director Asim Tanvir and senior producer Ryan Wolfe pulled back the curtain on what they’re calling a complete reimagining of the game’s approach to horror. The headline additions include redesigned ghosts, overhauled audio, expanded lore, new interactions, and a fresh approach to scares designed to unsettle both newcomers and players with thousands of hours logged.

For a game that’s been in Early Access since 2020, that’s a surprisingly ambitious goal. "We want to give them that fresher experience," Tanvir says when discussing Phasmophobia's veteran community. "We have members of our community who’ve been playing since day one. Some have racked up thousands of hours. We want to make sure when 1.0 launches, it feels fresh."

That seems to guide nearly every change coming to the game. The most immediate difference will be the ghosts themselves. Current Phasmophobia ghosts have become iconic, but Tanvir acknowledges what many longtime players have joked about for years: they don't always feel particularly ghostly. Many resemble zombies or generic horror monsters more than supernatural entities. The redesigns aim to change that.

Rather than a collection of shambling figures, the new ghosts are being built around the stories of their deaths. One drowned spirit appears suspended in an underwater haze, her hair drifting as if submerged. Another bears the scars of electrocution. Others reflect deaths by fire, falls, or other tragic ends. Then there's the child.

During the presentation, Kinetic Games showed me a work-in-progress horror showcase featuring one of the redesigned ghosts. The child repeatedly slammed her head into a wall before suddenly leaping onto the ceiling and crawling directly toward the screen. It was deeply unpleasant, but also great.

This was at 8am for me, by the way, and I hadn’t even had my coffee yet. I wasn’t ready for a ghost child to scare the jeebies out of me before breakfast.

The sequence felt like something pulled from a modern horror film. That's exactly what Kinetic Games appears to be aiming for. "We want them to be scared," Tanvir says when I ask what he hopes players feel when they boot up Phasmophobia 1.0 for the first time. "Whether you're playing solo or with friends, I want the audio, the interactions, everything to come together and for players to be completely scared."

If the ghost redesigns are the most visible changes, the audio overhaul may be the most impactful. Kinetic Games is rebuilding Phasmophobia's soundscape using Wwise, an industry-standard audio system that allows for more realistic sound propagation. In practical terms, that means sound will behave more like it does in the real world.

Voices will travel differently depending on walls, floors, doors, and materials. Rain tapping against a nearby window will sound different from rain falling outside a distant room. Players will be able to better pinpoint sounds around them, including noises coming from behind them which might hint towards nearby supernatural entities. Even proximity voice chat is getting an upgrade, meaning your friends will soon be able to realistically hear you panic from the other side of the house.

During one demonstration, a player spoke from an upper floor while another listened below. Instead of the muffled audio currently found in-game, the voice travels through the environment, bouncing through openings before reaching the listener.

Realizing the full vision, however, is going to take more time than Kinetic Games originally anticipated. During the presentation, the studio confirmed that Phasmophobia 1.0 has shifted from its previously planned 2026 release window to the second half of 2027 . For a game that has spent nearly six years in Early Access, it’s a big delay. "We’ve made this choice to ensure the highest quality launch when we do launch 1.0," Tanvir says.

The additional development time will allow the team to continue refining the new horror systems, audio technology, ghost redesigns, lore content, and the Nintendo Switch 2 version planned alongside the full release. "This is kind of the culmination of, if [original creator Daniel Knight] was given the time to do everything he had wanted to do in the beginning, this is what it would have looked like," Tanvir says.

items.title: Phasmaphobia Is Getting A 13 Willow Street Rework, And A New 1.0 Timeline
items.content:
Good news for multiplayer horror game fans: Phasmaphobia is getting an update next month.

The 13 Willow Street map rework will be out on July 21st for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series, and will be priced at US$19.99.

In addition, developer Kinetic Games has shared a roadmap for the game’s path towards a 1.0 release. The game’s full out-of-early-access release will be in 2027.

Kinetic Games has announced its updated 2026 roadmap for Phasmophobia, giving ghost hunters a fresh look at upcoming seasonal events, quality-of-life improvements, and a new 1.0 release window slated for the second half of 2027.

Shaped by community feedback and the team’s own ambitions for the game, the updated roadmap reflects Kinetic Games’ focus on refining core systems, improving player experience, and ensuring Phasmophobia reaches the level of quality players expect before leaving Early Access. Leading the roadmap is the highly anticipated 13 Willow Street map rework, planned for 21st July.

Following complete overhauls to the iconic Grafton and Bleasdale farmhouse, and most recently, 6 Tanglewood Drive, Willow is next on the list to be brought up to the team’s ideal development standards. This will include room refreshes, tweaked hiding spots, and integrated, never-before-seen lore, and will be accompanied by the first of two major quality-of-life updates that will bring improvements to player character models, animations and customisation options.

After Willow’s rework, players can expect a second quality-of-life update, followed by the game’s upcoming Unity 6 migration. Fan-favourite seasonal haunts including Crimson Eye and Winter’s Jest will also be returning for 2026, and ghost hunters can also look forward to Edgefield map rework, slated for December.

Phasmophobia’s updated roadmap prioritises quality over speed, with adjusted timelines enabling the team to deliver its vision for the game’s full 1.0 launch in 2027. Planned features include a complete overhaul of the game’s ghost models, replacing the current roster with a cast of unique ghosts that each have their own appearance, animations, and backstory.

The 1.0 launch will also expand Phasmophobia’s lore through continued environmental storytelling, introducing ghost-specific conduits and deeper connections to the people and places behind each haunting. Alongside these additions, significant audio improvements are planned for 1.0, including enhancements to environmental effects, weather systems, and spatial sound to create a more immersive investigation experience.

Over the past six years, Kinetic Games has grown from a solo developer into a studio of passionate artists, programmers, and designers dedicated to improving Phasmophobia. As development continues, the team remains focused on delivering the strongest possible version of the game ahead of its 1.0 launch and Nintendo Switch debut in the second half of 2027.

“I can only thank the community for the opportunities the game has given us, growing the studio to a team of over 50 people, and allowing us to work on improving Phasmophobia every day. Everyone’s feedback following our first Player Character Update showed that we have many areas to address before moving further along our roadmap. We’re going to take this time to improve and expand this update to shape the game into the refined horror experience our players expect,” said Daniel Knight, CEO and Game Director at Kinetic Games.

“We’re also investing more time into our 1.0 update, allowing us to add more content, further improve existing features, and ensure it launches at the highest standard possible. From the beginning, my vision for Phasmophobia has been to create a unique and immersive co-op horror experience. As the studio has grown, so has our ability to realise that vision, and we’re excited to keep building on it until we can deliver the experience our players deserve.”

For more information on Phasmophobia’s updated roadmap, visit the Kinetic Games website.

Your email address will not be published. Required fields are marked *

items.title: Preview: Phasmophobia 1.0's New Audio Overhaul Digs Its Heels into Pure Horror
items.summary: Coming soon, Phasmophobia will be seeing a new audio system that's more realistic, immersive, and outright scary.
items.content:
By Elena Chapella Published Jun 24, 2026, 10:00 AM EDT Elena is a Contributing Writer at DualShockers specializing in horror games, survival horror, open-world RPGs, fantasy, and historical fiction. She began covering games professionally in 2024, with her early gaming focus tied to Baldur’s Gate 3 before her interest in horror coverage grew through games like Silent Hill 2 Remake and Silent Hill f .

Generate a summary of this story follow Follow followed Followed Like Like Thread Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap It was six years ago when Phasmophobia was released, taking YouTube by storm and bringing players together for a fun horror experience we'd been itching for: paranormal investigation. In this span of six years, the game has evolved and continuously exploded in popularity while still in Early Access -- and finally, Phasmophobia 1.0 has been announced for 2027.

Kinetic Games originally had the game's official release planned for the end of this year, but given its updates, they wanted to make sure that players would be happy with the game's quality. And there are many, many new and exciting things coming to Phasmophobia 1.0 .

Sometimes, all a game needs is five minutes to keep you for life.

While you will still be investigating paranormal activity with your camera , you're going to experience the game completely differently with a complete audio overhaul, one that prioritizes realism to really get players immersed. Not just this, but players will also notice lore added in with the ghosts, revamps of their favorite maps , and other new mechanics.

Kinetic recently showed this to us in person at Summer Game Fest and let me just say: this makes me very, very excited for the official release.

Every horror fan knows that you genuinely cannot have a genuinely terrifying horror experience without good sound design , which is exactly what Kinetic Games is striving for. Every single sound is getting a revamp, with an added randomness to the sounds, so players aren't hearing the same thing over and over.

The immersion stems from the directionality of each sound — gone are the days of your friends' voices being muffled from inside the house despite the front door being wide open; instead, their voices will properly match the distance and volume as it would in the real world. Even while indoors, the sounds will bounce off corners with an echo rather than coming right through the wall, with a slight shift in the reverb depending on the materials present in the room (everything sounds different bouncing off stone rather than wood, for example).

Basically, you have to be within the direction of a sound in order to pick it up in the first place (otherwise it would just be an echo from afar), and the sounds themselves are being replaced with something more realistic. The audio is also reactive to what developers call Player State, Ghost State, and Environment State — all the individual aspects of the gameplay that will determine your overall experience.

Of course, this isn't even mentioning ambient noises, such as dead air in empty rooms and hallways or hearing raindrops on the roof; and I am a sucker for good rain sounds.

The Master of Horror himself may not have many games directly based on his work, but there are plenty of games directly inspired by his work instead.

And if you're playing with others (as most Phasmophobia players do), the quality of voice chat is also tweaked to be better, especially when paired with the in-game audio. Voice chat will be given the same treatment as all the other audio, given special care to what players will hear depending on distance, reverb, etc. The sound alone is going to change everything in the co-op experience, and it's going to be so much cooler than what players are already used to.

By just making the hilarious doppler effect you can experience with your friends all the more realistic, every scare is intensified; and while it would still be funny to hear your comrades crying out for their digital lives, it will still give you chills.

Simply put, the infamous Horror 2.0 update that Kinetic Games was talking about is the audio overhaul — because good sound is just that essential to a horror experience. The game is leagues of fun with friends, but it's a horror game first and foremost, so you're going to be terrified while having the time of your life, even more so with these new updates.

items.title: Phasmophobia 1.0 Aims for Unprecedented Immersion and Fear
items.summary: Phasmophobia's 1.0 update aims to terrify us with new ghosts, audio improvements, and more.
items.content:
Phasmophobia was always a bit of a dream game for me, as someone too meek to actually go out and search for ghosts, and much more content staying in the comfort of my own, non-haunted home. Needless to say, I put hundreds of hours into Phasmophobia when it first released in Early Access back in 2020. After what seems like a lifetime, it's finally nearing its highly anticipated 1.0 launch, promising to up the scares to a whole new level.

To that end, we attended a hands-off preview session at Summer Game Fest 2026, which first went over the details on a rework for Willow Street -- an iconic location from the game -- and finally what we can expect to see for the 1.0 release, slated for 2027.

Willow Street - Cat Trees and Horrifying Paintings So, it's been a hot minute since I've played Phasmophobia . My usual group fell apart, and this is certainly a game that benefits from having a party of friends to play with. As I understand it, several locations from the base game were reworked, and Willow Street is next on the list.

The original Willow Street, along with other locations in Phasmophobia, used pre-made, purchased assets. That was when developer Kinetic Games was a one-man show, but now it has dozens of developers at its disposal. These reworks are hand-made with new assets while also retaining the "spirit" of the original location, no pun intended.

I always enjoyed the residential houses in Phasmophobia more than any other location, so to see Willow Street getting a rework is great. This rework has a notable Palm Springs vibe in the outdoors portion, and upon entering the house, we see that it's a very lived-in building with a story to tell.

Cat trees. They're everywhere. On the walls, around the room. Whoever lives here really loves cats. I never really thought much about the stories of locations when investigating them, but with so much more detail and character, it brings Willow Street to life.

By and large, the floor plan is the same, though the rooms themselves are different. The cat-oriented room, for instance, but also the kitchen with its island counter. Going into the garage, it's a full-on gym with equipment like weights and a treadmill, while the former storage room is now a shower.

Going into the basement, it has a distinct man cave vibe, with a big TV, a beautiful sectional, and even a full-service bar. There are some hiding places scattered around to avoid ghosts, but with Willow Street being a smaller house in general, tense counters are almost a certainty.

The most terrifying room of the house is no doubt the atelier, formerly the boys' bedroom. It is filled with sinister, dark paintings, showing that the artist is clearly a disturbed individual. And this seems to be the general vibe Kinetic Games is going for with their reworks and the future of Phasmophobia. They want locations to tell a story with bits and details, but they also want players to interpret it for themselves and unravel the mysteries behind the hauntings.

1.0 Aims for a AAA Audio Experience Speaking of the future, 1.0 is on the horizon. This is where Phasmophobia' s Summer Game Fest presentation really impressed me. We take a surprisingly deep, sophisticated look at some new gameplay features and enhancements that will definitely add to the horror element of the game. Namely, audio.

Sound was a large focus here, and CEO and Game Director Daniel Knight assures me that they are aiming for AAA-quality sound design. This is achieved by completely revamping and replacing all of the pre-existing audio in Phasmophoia. Kinetic Games aims for dynamic sound that reacts to the state of the game, spatial audio, and more.

To highlight a few impressive details, Knight mentions "in-depth randomization," which is to ensure that no two sounds are exactly the same, just like real life. Sound will also now react differently depending on the material properties of the room. The wooden nature of the rustic Bleasdale Farmhouse will be distinctly different from the cold interiors of the Prison.

Vocal communication is also a key element to Phasmophobia , so when you're talking with a friend, it's going to sound as realistic as possible. The voice chat is now processed by acoustics, so the screams of your wimpiest buddy will now reverberate around a location.

Redesigned, Creepier Ghosts 1.0 also promises to bring a redesign for Phasmophobia 's iconic ghosts. While we've grown to love (or loathe) these phantoms, they are receiving redesigns to fit more cohesively with the game's narrative updates. The ghosts will be more distinct and are more or less their own characters, now.

items.title: You Aren’t Prepared For How Scary Phasmophobia 1.0 Is Meant To Be
Phasmophobia's Updated 2026 Roadmap Reveals 13 Willow Street Release Date, 1.0 Launch Delayed to 2027
items.summary: Phasmophobia's 13 Willow Street rework arrives on July 21 and will be followed by several updates, a reworked Edgefield map, and the game's 1.0 version, which is now slated for the second half of 2027.
items.content:
Publisher and developer Kinetic Games today unveiled an updated 2026 roadmap for hit co-op horror game Phasmophobia , detailing the events, patches, and map reworks slated to drop during 2026, while also confirming that its 1.0 version is now expected to release at some point during the second half of 2027.

Phasmophobia’s updated 2026 roadmap kicks off on July 21, 2026, when its long-awaited 13 Willow Street map rework will go live. It promises “room refreshes, tweaked hiding spots, and integrated, never-before-seen lore.” Accompanying it, the first of two planned quality of life updates improves player character models, animations, and customization options.

Content Promotion: If you’re after a different kind of horror mystery while waiting for Phasmophobia’s 1.0 launch, The Polar Darkness is also leaning into investigation, folklore, and atmospheric dread, swapping ghost-hunting co-op for a cult mystery in snowy 1980s Finland.

Phasmophobia Unveils Updated 2026 Roadmap, 1.0 Version Now Releases in 2027

August brings us the second quality-of-life update, while October marks the title’s migration to the Unity 6 engine. The Crimson Eye and Winter’s Jest seasonal events are also confirmed for 2026.

In December, just before the year ends, Phasmophobia’s Edgefield map will also be reworked. Here is a breakdown of Phasmophobia’s updated 2026 roadmap:

Content Release Date 13 Willow Street Rework + Quality of Life Update 1 July 21, 2026 Quality of Life Update 1 August 2026 Crimson Eye Event + Unity 6 Migration October 2026 Player Character Visual Update November 2026 Edgefield Rework + Winter’s Jest Event December 2026 1.0 Version Release + Nintendo Switch 2 Launch Second half of 2027

Phasmophobia’s 1.0 launch in 2027 promises a complete overhaul of the existing ghost models, which includes distinct appearances, animations, and backstories.

The title’s lore will also be expanded “through continued environmental storytelling, introducing ghost-specific conduits and deeper connections to the people and places behind each haunting.”

Audio improvements are also part of developer Kinetic Games’ plans, particularly targeting environmental effects, weather systems, and spatial sound, hoping to create an even more immersive experience.

“I can only thank the community for the opportunities the game has given us, growing the studio to a team of over 50 people, and allowing us to work on improving Phasmophobia every day. Everyone’s feedback following our first Player Character Update showed that we have many areas to address before moving further along our roadmap. We’re going to take this time to improve and expand this update to shape the game into the refined horror experience our players expect,” said Daniel Knight, CEO and Game Director at Kinetic Games.

“We’re also investing more time into our 1.0 update, allowing us to add more content, further improve existing features, and ensure it launches at the highest standard possible. From the beginning, my vision for Phasmophobia has been to create a unique and immersive co-op horror experience. As the studio has grown, so has our ability to realise that vision, and we’re excited to keep building on it until we can deliver the experience our players deserve.”

Although Phasmophobia’s 1.0 version delay is bound to disappoint some of its most dedicated fans, the 13 Willow Street and Edgefield map reworks alongside all the other bits included in its updated 2026 roadmap should give us enough reasons to return to the co-op horror title over the coming months.

Phasmophobia is available in early access on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S.

Enter your email address to subscribe to WTMG and receive notifications of new posts by email.

Way Too Many Games is an up and coming gaming site run by gamers for gamers. Established in January 2017 Way Too Many Games began with the goal to provide objective reviews and better represent independent developers and niche titles, eventually expanding into board games as well. Since its launch, WTMG has been a presence at E3, Brasil Game Show, and Play NYC, with more events on the way.

At Way Too Many Games we feature a transparent review method divided into four segments: Graphics (25% of the final score), Gameplay (25%), Sound (10%), and Fun Factor (40%). Scores are always rounded to the nearest multiple of 0.5.

items.title: Phasmophobia - Hotfix v0.17.1.5
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we have released a hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing two issues raised by the community this week. The update is now available across all platforms. Patch notes: Fixed an issue that caused players to bec
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we have released a hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing two issues raised by the community this week. The update is now available across all platforms.

Patch notes:

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to become softlocked when using the Spirit Box while using a controller.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue for Steam players that caused Voice Recognition to not work.

    [/*]

  • If you are encountering issues with Voice Recognition on the "Voice" setting on PC, please ensure that the default microphone assigned on your PC is the microphone specified in your windows settings.

    [/*]

items.title: Phasmophobia v0.17.1.4 - Patch Notes
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we have released our latest update for Phasmophobia, addressing several known issues in the game. We are aware of the current ongoing issues in-game and are currently implementing fixes and improvements in
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we have released our latest update for Phasmophobia, addressing several known issues in the game.

We are aware of the current ongoing issues in-game and are currently implementing fixes and improvements in future updates for Phasmophobia. We’ll be sharing our plans for the rest of 2026 later this month with you all, including a timeline for fixes and updates related to both the Player Character Update and game in general. In the meantime, please continue to provide feedback and bug reports so that our team can investigate as much as possible. You can submit any and all issues via our Support Centre.

New

  • The VR belt has been reimplemented following player feedback. Players will now also be able to switch between having their Journal and Walkie Talkie on the belt or their shoulder. 

    • The VR belt will only be visible locally, players who are looking at a VR player will see the equipment on their body instead.

      [/*]
    [/*]

Changes

  • The line of sight ghost speed accelerations implemented in the Player Character Update have been reverted to their original state.

    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to become softlocked when using the Spirit Box before interacting with the Truck’s CCTV.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to kill players through floors.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to kill players outside.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 3 Firelight to not work correctly if placed while lit.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where player jackets would appear incorrectly when loading into a Multiplayer lobby.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where pressing ‘Join’ in Multiplayer without having a code entered would cause issues creating lobbies, and remove audio from the game.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where VR players were unable to use Text Input for the Spirit Box, Monkey Paw and Ouija Board.

    [/*]

Known Issues

  • Players across all platforms are experiencing issues with voice recognition/voice chat. A fix is being investigated.

    [/*]

Please note: Following this update, players on Windows 7/8 or other out-of-date Windows installs may need to run Windows Update (and restart) for voice chat to connect. This is due to a recent security certificate update on our voice servers. More info can be found here.

As you continue your investigations in Phasmophobia, if you encounter bugs, issues or simply have feedback for our team, please use our Support Centre to submit your reports.

We hope you enjoy.

items.content:
Be a part of the story with The Seer ID Card & Badge.

Our Phasmophobia by Alan Wake Twitch Drop will be available from 12PM BST May 19th - 12PM BST May 26th.

Earn the Animated variant via Kinetic Games Partner streams, and the Standard via any Phasmophobia streams during the campaign. The total watch time to earn the Drops is 2 hours.

items.title: Phasmophobia - Hotfix v0.17.1.1
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we have released a small hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing feedback from the community following our latest update. Patch notes can be found below. Fixes Fixed an issue that caused held equipment/items t
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we have released a small hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing feedback from the community following our latest update.

Patch notes can be found below.

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused held equipment/items to visually stutter when moving.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several issues that lead to game crashes.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Kormos to detect dead players and attempt to kill them.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Kormos could detect sound from dead players.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Coffee World tent names were incorrect.

    [/*]

  • Other fixes and improvements.

    [/*]

Known Issues

  • Players can become locked when interacting with the Truck's CCTV screen while using a Spirit Box.

    [/*]
